title: How The CleanSpark (CLSK) Story Is Shifting As Analyst Targets And Assumptions Diverge
text: The average analyst target for CleanSpark moved marginally from $19.21 to $19.29 per share, though this modest adjustment masks deeper disagreement among researchers about the company's valuation prospects. Keefe Bruyette elevated its price target to $16 from $14, maintaining an Outperform rating. Northland praised management's capital allocation approach, describing the preferred payout strategy as a smart move, signaling confidence in how leadership balances shareholder returns with growth funding. Cantor Fitzgerald reduced its target by $3, reflecting skepticism about valuation despite bullish moves elsewhere. This reduction underscores disagreement regarding execution risk and how much growth potential is already reflected in current share pricing. CleanSpark reported April 2026 Bitcoin production of 640 coins, with peak single-day output of 22.38 Bitcoin and average daily production of 21.33 Bitcoin, continuing consistent output from prior months. Analysts adjusted long-term revenue growth expectations downward from 8.70% to 8.31%, while improving net profit margin assumptions from 11.33% to 11.73%.
